name: BashTool
description: Execute a shell command in the workspace directory. Use for running tests, installs, builds, git operations, and any other terminal commands.

name: FileReadTool
description: Read the contents of a file. Returns file content with line numbers. Use this before editing a file to understand its current state.

file_path.description: Path to the file, relative to the workspace root

name: FileWriteTool
description: Create a new file or completely overwrite an existing file with new content. Use when creating files from scratch. For small targeted edits to existing files, prefer FileEditTool instead.

file_path.description: Path to the file, relative to the workspace root

content.description: The full content to write to the file

name: FileEditTool
description: Make a targeted edit to an existing file by replacing an exact string with a new string. The old_string must appear exactly once in the file. Always use FileReadTool first to see the current content.

file_path.description: Path to the file, relative to the workspace root

old_string.description: The exact text to find and replace (must exist exactly once in the file)

new_string.description: The new text to replace old_string with

name: GlobTool
description: Find files matching a glob pattern. Use to discover what files exist in the workspace. Example patterns: **/*.ts, src/**/*.py, **/package.json

pattern.description: Glob pattern to match files (e.g. **/*.ts, src/**/*.py)

base_path.description: Subdirectory to search from, relative to workspace root (optional)

name: GrepTool
description: Search file contents using a regex pattern. Returns matching lines with file path and line number. Use to find where code, strings, or patterns are used.

path.description: Directory or file to search in, relative to workspace root (optional, defaults to entire workspace)

name: ListDirectoryTool
description: List the files and subdirectories in a directory. Use to explore the workspace structure.

path.description: Directory path, relative to the workspace root

name: WebFetchTool
description: Fetch the content of a web page or URL. Use to retrieve documentation, check APIs, or read external resources.

name: TodoWriteTool
description:
Create or update the todo list for the current task. Use this at the start of any multi-step task to plan your work, then update it as you complete each step. The todo list is shown to the user so they can track your progress.

Todo item status values:
- 'pending': not started yet
- 'in_progress': currently working on (max 1 at a time)
- 'done': completed successfully
- 'blocked': cannot proceed, needs user input

Always update to 'in_progress' before starting a step, and 'done' immediately after completing it.

todos.description: Complete list of todo items. Must include ALL items — both existing and new.
